items.briefing:
Microsoft has signed a formal agreement with the Australian Government to deepen cooperation on cloud security and AI, signalling stronger protections for government data and services. Separately, North Korea-linked hackers have been targeting software developers through GitHub — if your organisation uses developers or contractors who work with open-source code repositories, they should be cautious about unexpected or unfamiliar code contributions.

Microsoft has also released a record-breaking number of security patches this month. All Australian organisations running Windows or other Microsoft products should prioritise installing these updates as soon as possible to protect against known vulnerabilities.

**Tip:** Check that automatic updates are enabled on all work devices today.

featured.title: Microsoft, Australian Government sign MOU on cloud, cybersecurity and AI cooperation
featured.quote: Microsoft-Australian Government cybersecurity partnership signals major national digital infrastructure collaboration.
